summary: The live status summary is strongly guide-aligned, and all three prior guide-alignment findings are resolved this iteration: Phases::Advance now wraps its advance writes in ApplicationRecord.transaction (backend-guide 'Services' atomicity); ManagerTick and Advance now defer every BroadcastColumn/BroadcastStatus behind ActiveRecord.after_all_transactions_commit (backend-guide after-commit rule); and the gate approve button/note input now match the shared primary-button and form-input specs (ui-style-guide 'Core components'). Pipelines::StatusSummary remains a correctly-placed pure derivation PORO in app/lib with a query-style .for, tone sourced only from the single StatusHelper::STATUS_TONES table (dot and badge cannot diverge), broadcasts target the smallest DOM unit (dom_id(pipeline,:summary)/dom_id(phase,:column)), both surfaces render true state on load with streams as enhancement, and the summary is an aria-live=polite region with the sentence (not color) carrying meaning. One deviation blocks a pass: the human gate-approval path never broadcasts the pipeline status, violating 'Live by default' for non-approving viewers when a gate stops waiting. Two minor items: business branching in ApprovalsController, and a stray tmp_run_tests.rb committed at repo root.

findings.issue: ui-style-guide.md 'Principles -> Live by default': 'Anything that changes server-side (step progress, heartbeats, phase status) updates in place via Turbo Streams — never require a manual refresh.' Pipelines::BroadcastStatus is called from StepRuns::Claim/RecordProgress/Complete and Phases::ManagerTick, but NOT on the human-approval path: ApprovalsController#create -> Phases::Approve -> Phases::Advance transitions the pipeline out of awaiting_human (to running or completed) and broadcasts only phase columns via BroadcastColumn — a grep of app/ shows no Pipelines::BroadcastStatus call on this path. Consequently the prominent detail-page summary and every compact list-row summary on OTHER open pages keep showing 'Waiting on human approval at the <phase> gate' until a manual reload; only the approving user is masked by the controller's redirect. This is exactly the phase-status change the summary exists to surface. Fix: call Pipelines::BroadcastStatus.call(pipeline) after commit on the approve/advance path (mirroring ManagerTick's tail call and its after_all_transactions_commit deferral). backend-guide.md 'Real-time (Turbo) conventions' — 'Broadcasts happen from services (after commit)' — points to Advance/Approve as the correct home for it.

backend-guide.md 'Controllers — light, always': 'No business branching in controllers — a controller may branch only on result.success?' The success branch builds its flash with `phase.pipeline.reload.completed?`, which both branches on domain state beyond result.success? and issues a reload query to rediscover an outcome the service already produced. Phases::Approve returns Result.success(@phase) without surfacing whether the pipeline completed, forcing the controller to reach back into the model. Surface the completion outcome from the service Result (e.g. return the advanced pipeline / a completed flag) and let the controller read it, rather than re-deriving domain state in the controller.

A stray test-runner scaffold (tmp_run_tests.rb) is committed at the repo root; it manually preloads ActionCable and requires every test file to work around a local boot-order quirk. It is not part of the feature and is unreferenced by app or test code. No specific guide rule names it, but it contradicts backend-guide.md 'General Rails practice' — 'Prefer boring, obvious code ... Optimize for the next reader' — as shipped clutter that should not merge. Remove it from the branch (keep any needed runner outside version control or under bin/).

ui-style-guide.md 'Color': 'text `gray-900` / `gray-500`.' The compact (list-row) summary text uses `text-sm text-gray-700`, an off-palette neutral not in the sanctioned text set. Use gray-900 (primary) or gray-500 (secondary) to keep both surfaces on the defined scale; the detail form already uses text-gray-900 correctly.
